summary: axois-utils ships system-info exfiltration module with hardcoded C2
details: The package name is a one-character typosquat of the popular `axios` HTTP client. The package ships a file `distrube.js` (itself a misspelling of 'distribute') that imports `os`, `fs`, `child_process`, and `https`, collects host identifiers (hostname, username) at lines 197/228/275/305 and 278, and issues outbound HTTPS POST/GET requests (lines 116, 200, 209, 291, 308) to hardcoded destinations. The combination of OS/filesystem/child_process imports with hardcoded HTTPS POSTs carrying hostname and username fields is the canonical system-info exfiltration shape, and the typosquat name on a top-tier npm target indicates intent to be installed by mistake by developers reaching for `axios`. Installing or loading this package risks leaking host identity and arbitrary command execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
